name: Kang-Ped Bhet-Yang - Thai Roast Duck Red Curry.
recipeInstructions:
1. Small Thai "eggplants" belong to the eggplant family but don't resemble US eggplants. These are about the size of large green peas and look similar. Regular green peas can be used as a substitute.
2. Combine all red curry paste ingredients in a mortar and pound until well mixed into a paste. You may use commercially available paste if available, adjusting to taste.
3. Debone the roast duck and cut meat into bite-sized rectangular pieces (with skin on). Cut neck and wing into pieces.
4. Place coconut milk in a large saucepan and heat until boiling. Add duck pieces and cook until tender.
5. Put coconut cream into a frying pan, add red curry paste. Heat over high heat, stirring constantly, until all the paste is mixed in and thoroughly heated.
6. Add Kaffir Lime leaves, fish sauce (to taste), coconut sugar, and continue to heat (keep stirring continuously to prevent burning) until small oil forms on the surface of the sauce.
7. Add the sauce to the saucepan with duck and coconut milk and bring back to a boil. Add all remaining ingredients except basils, which are added when the curry starts to boil. Remove from heat and serve with plain boiled white rice.
